name: Kung Pau's Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Chicken, breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
2 teaspoons Rice wine, or dry sherry
2 tablespoons Peanut oil, or corn oil
1 Bell pepper, seeded and cut into
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Roast peanuts until golden brown (about 10 minutes). Remove and set aside.
2. To make the chicken marinade, mix together rice wine, cornstarch, and 1 teaspoon sesame oil in a bowl. Toss chicken pieces with the marinade; set aside until ready to cook.
3. In another small bowl, combine soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, and 1 teaspoon sesame oil. Heat a wok over medium heat until hot.
4. Add salt and dried red chiles to the hot wok; cook for about 15 seconds until chiles are charred.
5. Add ginger and garlic; stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant and lightly browned.
6. Increase heat to high, add bell pepper pieces, and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
7. Add the marinated chicken to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute or until the pieces are firm to touch and opaque white in color. Do not overcook.
8. Stir in the sauce mixture; toss until it thickens into a glaze (about 30 seconds).
9. Remove wok from heat, add the reserved roasted peanuts, and gently fold everything together.
10. Serve hot with other dishes. Serves 4 to 6.
